Bicolored Striped Sweat Bee

  1. Home
  3. Gallery
  4. Animals
  5. Insects
  6. Bees
  7. Bicolored Striped Sweat Bee

Emerald in the Spiderwort

Golden Cargo

Pollen Laden

Among Violet Filaments

Lifting from the Bloom

Bicolored Striped Sweat Bee on a Flower

Agopostemon virescens

The Bicolored Striped Sweat Bee is a small, striking halictid with a metallic blue-green head and thorax and, in the female, a black abdomen crossed by pale bands. In these photographs, a pollen-laden female works among the violet filaments and yellow anthers of a spiderwort flower, the warm gold of the pollen contrasting with her iridescent green body.

For identification details and comparison with similar species, see Augochlorella striata in the Field Notes section.

Emerald in the Spiderwort
Golden pollen gathers as a metallic-green bee works the violet flower.
Camera: Canon EOS 5D Mark IV
Lens: EF100mm f/2.8L Macro IS USM
Settings: ISO 2000 • Aperture f/10.0 • Shutter 1/500 s
D79A0849 • Size: 3000 x 2000

Golden Cargo
Heavy with pollen, the metallic-green bee works its way through the violet flower.
Camera: Canon EOS 5D Mark IV
Lens: EF100mm f/2.8L Macro IS USM
Settings: ISO 2000 • Aperture f/10.0 • Shutter 1/500 s
D79A0851 • Size: 3000 x 2000

Pollen Laden
Pale abdominal bands and heavily provisioned hind legs identify a female at work.
Camera: Canon EOS 5D Mark IV
Lens: EF100mm f/2.8L Macro IS USM
Settings: ISO 2000 • Aperture f/10.0 • Shutter 1/500 s
D79A0852 • Size: 3000 x 2000

Among Violet Filaments
The bee presses into the flower’s center, dusted with newly gathered pollen.
Camera: Canon EOS 5D Mark IV
Lens: EF100mm f/2.8L Macro IS USM
Settings: ISO 2000 • Aperture f/10.0 • Shutter 1/500 s
D79A0849 • Size: 3000 x 2000

Lifting from the Bloom
Wings become a silver blur as the bee leaves the spiderwort flower.
Camera: Canon EOS 5D Mark IV
Lens: EF100mm f/2.8L Macro IS USM
Settings: ISO 2000 • Aperture f/10.0 • Shutter 1/500 s
D79A0857 • Size: 3000 x 2000
